Question: Find the least common denominator for the fractions: $$\frac{7}{12}$$ and $$\frac{11}{15} .$$
o3 Answer:
The least common denominator (LCD) is the least common multiple (LCM) of the two denominators, 12 and 15.

Prime-factorize the denominators
• 12 = 2² × 3  
• 15 = 3 × 5  

Form the LCM by taking every distinct prime factor to its highest power appearing in either factorization:
• 2² (from 12)  
• 3 (highest power is 3¹)  
• 5 (from 15)

LCM = 2² × 3 × 5 = 4 × 3 × 5 = 60

Therefore, the least common denominator of 7/12 and 11/15 is 60.

15 = 3·5  

The LCM must contain each prime to its highest power:  
LCM = 2²·3·5 = 4·3·5 = 60  

So the least common denominator is 60.  

If you rewrite each fraction over 60:  
7/12 = (7·5)/(12·5) = 35/60  
11/15 = (11·4)/(15·4) = 44/60
